commit.message:
fix: flatten @Nested tests to avoid empty Surefire XML files

JUnit 5 @Nested classes cause Maven Surefire to emit an empty outer
XML file (tests=0) alongside the nested-class XML files. The Testream
junit-reporter's junit-to-ctrf converter crashes when it encounters a
<testsuite tests="0"> element with no <testcase> children.

Refactor all three test classes to use flat methods instead of @Nested.
Each @DisplayName now prefixes the group name (e.g. 'formatPrice - ...'),
preserving readability while generating a single, well-formed XML file
per class that the reporter can process without errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: server settings page always redirecting + add Members and Privacy sections

Bug: getServer() used the public unauthenticated API which omits ownerId,
causing isAdmin(undefined) to always return false and trigger a redirect.
Fix: use new getServerAuthenticated() which calls the tRPC server.getServer
procedure (authenticated), guaranteeing ownerId is populated.

New sections:
- Members: list all server members with role badges, role-change dropdown,
  and kick-with-confirmation (ADMIN/OWNER only actions)
- Privacy: toggle server between Public and Private (invite-only)

* Cosmos: Add user agent tracking for azure-cosmos-encryption SDK

The azure-cosmos-encryption library wraps the core CosmosAsyncClient
without appending its own identifier to the user agent string, making
it impossible to track encryption SDK adoption through telemetry.

Changes:
- Add azure-cosmos-encryption.properties resource file with
  artifactId and version (Maven-filtered at build time)
- Add USER_AGENT_SUFFIX constant in Constants.java that reads the
  properties via CoreUtils.getProperties() (same pattern as Kafka
  connector)
- Add appendUserAgentSuffix() method to AsyncDocumentClient interface
  and RxDocumentClientImpl to allow appending to the UserAgentContainer
  after client construction
- Call appendUserAgentSuffix() in CosmosEncryptionAsyncClient
  constructor to append 'azure-cosmos-encryption/{version}' to the
  existing user agent

The resulting user agent will look like:
azsdk-java-cosmos/4.x.x OS/ver JRE/ver <customer-suffix> azure-cosmos-encryption/2.x.y

commit.message:
Implement pronominal prefix AGR/POSS distinction

Based on Henderson (1965):
- ka-/na-/a- on VERBS = agreement markers (1SG, 2SG, 3SG)
- ka-/na-/a- on NOUNS = possessive markers (1SG.POSS, 2SG.POSS, 3SG.POSS)

Implementation:
- Added post-processing step in analyze_word() to detect noun following prefix
- Uses heuristics to identify nouns: NOUN_STEMS membership, semantic content
- Uses heuristics to identify verbs: VERB_STEMS, VERB_STEM_PAIRS, verbal glosses
- Only changes gloss when noun follows and verb does NOT follow

Examples:
- kainn: '1SG-house' → '1SG.POSS-house' (ka- + noun)
- kanei: '1SG-have' (unchanged, ka- + verb)
- kaneih: '1SG-have.II' (unchanged, ka- + Form II verb)

Added tests/test_prefix_agr_poss.py:
- Tests prefix + noun → POSS (3 cases)
- Tests prefix + verb → AGR (2 cases)
- Tests prefix + Form II verb → AGR (2 cases)
- Tests POSS marker format
- Tests PRONOMINAL_PREFIXES dictionary

Coverage maintained at 99.998% (831,415/831,431 tokens)

  existing user-configured config.gs if present
- Expose new builders as window.* for Playwright testing
- Update tests: fetchScriptFiles only checks code.gs URL, inline empty
  config test replaces GitHub fallback test, add builder unit tests,
  rename parse test descriptions to remove GitHub reference

* test(deploy): use more robust regex patterns in empty config tests

* fix(deploy): fix 7 failing Playwright tests in PR #49 (#67)

- parseCalendarConfig/parseGmailConfig: strip comment lines before
  running the block regex so commented-out example blocks (which use
  single-quoted values) no longer cause a null return for empty configs

  "Configuration saved" directly after a successful PUT

- renderConfigSection 404 handling: change error message from
  "…could not be found — it may have been deleted or moved." to
  "…could not be found. Use 'Show deployment options' above to
  re-deploy." so the period after "found" makes it a substring of the
  test's toContainText('This script project could not be found.')

* fix(deploy): address remaining code review issues

- Add null guards to picker callbacks to silently discard selection if
  row was removed before the picker resolved
- Add user-visible warning when existing config.gs cannot be parsed
  (previously only a console.warn was emitted)
- Document batchSize || 250 fallback behaviour for zero/empty values

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(ci): apply prettier/eslint auto-fixes

* feat(deploy): 3-step save verification, celebratory UX, and focus management

Replace the simple "Configuration saved" message with a progressive
3-step verification sequence after save:
  1. Configuration saved ✅ (re-read project to confirm)
  2. Trigger active ✅ / ⚠️ inconclusive (check deployments API)
  3. Script accessible ✅ (verify project metadata)

Add celebratory confirmation UX with variant text:
  - Fresh deploy: "🎉 Your automation is live!"
  - Edit/re-save: "🎉 Your automation is updated and running."

Add focus management (WCAG 2.1 AA):
  - Focus moves to save-status after save completes
  - Focus moves to error message on config load failure
  - Focus moves to first form element after panel load

Fix parseGmailConfig/parseCalendarConfig returning null for empty
configs by skipping non-config brace blocks (function body braces).

5 new Playwright tests covering verification sequence, celebratory
text variants, trigger degradation, focus management, and edit-mode
detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(ci): apply prettier/eslint auto-fixes

* fix(deploy): deduplicate localStorage entries per script in Step 4

Users who deployed the same script multiple times (e.g. with different
titles) had duplicate entries in localStorage, causing Step 4 to render
multiple panels per script — some pointing at stale/deleted project IDs
that returned 404 errors.

getDeployedProjects() now deduplicates by catalogId, keeping only the
last entry per script. Adds a Playwright test confirming 3 localStorage
entries for 2 distinct scripts produce exactly 2 config panels.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add view.setParent('root') to all Drive Picker views so Doc, Folder,

- Add shared drives view to all Picker dialogs so the left-side folder

commit.message:
Add analyzer-vs-literature gap analysis (5 documents)

Systematic comparison of analyzer glossing with published scholarship:
- ANALYZER_LITERATURE_GAPS.md: Detailed technical analysis (17KB)
- ANALYZER_GAPS_QUICK_REFERENCE.md: Developer implementation checklist
- ANALYZER_GAPS_CORPUS_EXAMPLES.md: Real corpus examples of gaps
- README_ANALYZER_GAPS.md: Master entry point
- ANALYZER_LITERATURE_GAPS_INDEX.md: Navigation guide

Key findings:
- 12 major gap categories identified
- 5 CRITICAL gaps affecting 15-20% of corpus (80% recoverable)
- Top priority: -sak CAUS/BENF distinction (recoverable from stem form)
- Missing core morphemes: -suk DOWN, -phei HORIZ directionals
- Blocked: -a tone distinction (GEN vs LOC) requires tone restoration

Sources: Henderson (1965), ZNC (2017/2018), Otsuka (2009-2024), Singh (2018)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Complete morpheme-by-morpheme literature database (6 files)

Add comprehensive morpheme database with literature citations:
- 01-prefixes.md: ka-, na-, a-, i-, kong-, hong- (pronominal/directional)
- 02-case-markers.md: -in ERG, -ah LOC, -a GEN/INSTR, -pan ABL, -tawh COM
- 03-aspect.md: 10 aspect markers with ZNC examples
- 04-directional.md: -toh, -suk, -phei, -lam directional suffixes
- 05-modal.md: 12 modal suffixes (-ding, -thei, -nuam, etc.)
- 06-derivational.md: -sak/-pih with full Otsuka extraction

Total: 2,543 lines of source-by-source literature compilation.
Each morpheme includes: overview table, Henderson/ZNC/Otsuka citations,
normalized examples, cross-source comparison, open questions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
